subject: Product Management
title: From Consumer Goods to Tech
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on From Consumer Goods to Tech.

hook.text: Hewlett-Packard brought the brand man concept to technology in the 1960s — and changed the role forever.

buildup.text: HP hired brand managers from P&G and gave them technical products instead of soap and toothpaste.

discovery.text: Tech products were different: engineers built them, but brand managers had to translate user needs into specs.

twist.text: The role split in two: marketing PMs who understood customers, and technical PMs who understood engineering.

climax.text: By the 1980s, every major tech company had product managers bridging business and engineering.

punchline.text: Tech didn't invent the PM role. It reinvented it.

quiz.question: How did the PM role change when it moved to tech?
options: PMs had to bridge business needs and engineering — a new challenge
It stayed exactly the same as at P&G
Tech companies eliminated the role entirely
